answer:
\boxed{H}

Incandescent lamps have the highest Colour Rendering Index (CRI) of **100**, closely followed by LEDs (typically 80–98). Xenon and halogen lamps also perform well (CRI ~90–95), but incandescent is the traditional benchmark. Low-pressure sodium (D) has the worst CRI (~0), while others like fluorescent (I) and metal halide (J) vary widely.
